title: dianthus cintranus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Dianthus cintranus subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western rock pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and grass-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Dianthus cintranus subsp. occidentalis include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
